ops.title: Raw semble MCP removed everywhere 2026-07-14 — ccx code search is the only sanctioned surface; semble reappearing anywhere is a regression
ops.body:
2026-07-14: the raw `semble` MCP server was de-exposed everywhere after claude-pool sessions kept calling `mcp__semble__search` instead of `ccx code search` (which never used the registered server — cc-context spawns its own `uvx --from "semble[mcp]>=0.5" semble` child). Removed from: `~/.claude.json` + all 27 `~/.cc-pool/accounts/*/.claude.json` copies, the `mcp__semble__*` allow rule in `~/.claude/settings.json`, project `.mcp.json` in claude-pool/captain-hook/cc-context (emptied to `{"mcpServers": {}}`), `enabledMcpjsonServers` in claude-pool + captain-hook settings.local.json, and the cc-transcript plugin (0.2.0 — it bundled a semble server at its plugin root; the only plugin that did). claude-pool's local AGENTS.md "Code Search" fragment (which taught raw `semble.search()`) was replaced by the shared `cc-skills:ccx` import, and claude-rules.md:19 now cites `ccx`, not `semble`.

**Regression signals**: `semble` in `claude mcp list`, `mcpServers.semble` back in any `~/.claude.json` (a pre-sweep live session flushing stale state can resurrect it — re-delete), a repo `.mcp.json` registering semble (repo-bootstrap `test_swift.py` asserts scaffolds don't), or cc-transcript plugin cache <0.2.0.

**Worktree index pollution** (the visible symptom): semble's walker reads only literal `.gitignore`/`.sembleignore` files, never `.git/info/exclude`, so `.claude/worktrees/*` copies polluted the shared per-repo index (`~/Library/Caches/semble/<sha256(path)>`), hitting ccx too. Fix: `.claude/worktrees/` in the tracked `.gitignore` (repo-bootstrap 0.54.0 base template + cc-skills + claude-pool); the index self-heals on next search via its file-set revalidation. Any repo using worktree isolation without that gitignore line will pollute again. See [[cc-guides-generated-claude-md]] for the fragment/render mechanics used.
